sections.paragraph:
ἐνισχύω fut. ἐνισχύσω LXX; 1 aor. ἐνίσχυσα, pass. ἐνισχύθην

① intr. (Aristot., Theophr., Diod S 18, 61, 3, LXX; PMerton 12, 10 [58 a.d.]; TestJob 47:7) to recover from loss of strength, grow strong, regain one’s strength (cp. Epict. 3, 24, 108; Gen 48:2; TestJob, TestSim; ApcMos 10) ἐνίσχυσεν Ac 9:19 (v.l. ἐνισχύθη). Cp. 19:20 D.

② trans. (Hippocr. et al. [Hobart 81]; 2 Km 22:40; Sir 50:4; PsSol 16:12f; TestSol 26:8 H [without obj.]; TestDan 6:5; Jos. Ant. 7, 269), to cause to recover from loss of strength, strengthen τινά Lk 22:43; B 14:7 (Is 42:6). τὶ urge someth. insistently MPol 17:2.—DELG s.v. ἰσχύς. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

intr. intr. = intransitive

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

trans. trans. = transitive

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hobart Hobart = WH., Medical Language of St. Luke—List 6

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

obj. obj. = object, objective

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

someth. someth. = something

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 337
